artifacts.summary: Official-domain discovery identified a university AI policy page as the best source for narrow staged claims. The official AI hub was retained as a low-specificity source, the practical guide PDF as supporting guidance, and a repository thesis was rejected because it is not institutional policy.

stopConditions: Stop after one official university-wide AI policy or guidance source supports narrow claim candidates.
Do not use repository theses, news, or generic hub pages as claim evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Comillas permits students to use AI as a reference tool when the use is acknowledged and cited, and directs students to check each course guide and consult the instructor for activity-specific rules.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Comillas says teachers who incorporate AI tools in their courses should ensure that the conditions for use are clearly established in the course guide.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Comillas states that using AI to create complete works or relevant parts without citing the source or tool, or without express permission in the assignment description, will be considered plagiarism under the university's general regulations.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Comillas prohibits entering restricted data into any generative AI tool or service without the corresponding approval, including protected or personal data not authorized by the DPO or not meeting security measures.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: El uso de la IA como herramienta de referencia está permitido siempre que sea reconocido y citado en cualquier trabajo presentado. Los estudiantes deben revisar la Guía Docente del curso y consultar con su profesor sobre el uso de la IA en cada una de las asignaturas y en cada una de sus actividades.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI reference use is allowed if acknowledged and cited; students should check the course guide and ask the instructor about each activity.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Como sucede con otras herramientas pedagógicas, los profesores que decidan incorporar herramientas de IA en sus cursos deben asegurarse de que las condiciones para su uso estén claramente establecidas en la Guía Docente.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Teachers who incorporate AI tools should make the conditions for use clear in the course guide.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: El uso de IA para crear trabajos completos o partes relevantes, sin citar la fuente o la herramienta o sin estar permitido expresamente en la descripción del trabajo, será considerado plagio y regulado conforme al Reglamento General de la Universidad.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Using AI to create complete works or relevant parts without citation or express permission will be treated as plagiarism under the general university regulations.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Está prohibido ingresar datos restringidos en cualquier herramienta o servicio de IA generativa sin la aprobación correspondiente. Esto incluye datos protegidos y personales no autorizados por el DPO o que no cumplan las medidas de seguridad.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Restricted data may not be entered into generative AI tools or services without approval, including protected or personal data not authorized by the DPO or not meeting security measures.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim because the source explicitly permits reference-tool use when acknowledged and cited, while keeping activity-level rules with course guides and instructors.
Approved with 'should' wording because the Spanish source says teachers who incorporate AI tools must ensure conditions are clear in the course guide.
Approved because the official page directly states the plagiarism treatment for uncited or unpermitted AI-generated complete works or relevant parts.
Approved because the source explicitly prohibits entering restricted data in generative AI without corresponding approval.

artifacts.title: Comillas Pontifical University AI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Comillas Pontifical University has an official Spanish-language AI guidance page covering student reference use and citation, course guide conditions for teacher-approved uses, plagiarism treatment for uncited or unpermitted AI-generated work, and restricted-data limits for generative AI.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged OpenClaw candidates and are not canonical public facts.
The evidence is based on a university-wide Spanish guidance page; it does not resolve every course-level rule or local guide-doc exception.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
